meta.name: Herb-Roasted Vegetables
meta.subtitle: Sheet-pan roasted vegetables with garlic, herbs, lemon, and olive oil (Herb & Garlic style)

source.name: ssa.cuisines.herb_garlic
notes: Canonical SSA Herb & Garlic roasted vegetables baseline.
Deterministic sheet-pan method with swap-friendly vegetable roles and oven timing bands.

yield.notes: Serves 6 as a side, or 3–4 as a main with grains/beans/protein.

required.label: Sheet pan
required.notes: Use a large sheet pan to avoid overcrowding (better browning).

optional.label: Parchment paper
optional.notes: Easier cleanup; browning is still good.

optional.label: Roasting rack
optional.notes: Optional; usually not necessary for vegetables.

ingredients.label: Broccoli florets
notes: Cut florets into bite-size pieces; keep stems sliced thin so they roast evenly.

ingredients.label: Zucchini, thick half-moons
notes: Cut 1/2-inch thick so it roasts instead of steaming.

ingredients.label: Carrots, sliced on a bias
notes: Cut similar thickness so they finish with the broccoli.

ingredients.label: Extra-virgin olive oil
notes: Use enough oil to coat; dry vegetables won’t brown as well.

ingredients.label: Garlic, minced (or grated)
notes: Add halfway through roasting if you prefer less risk of garlic scorching.

ingredients.label: Fresh rosemary, finely chopped (optional)
notes: Rosemary is potent—keep it fine and light.

steps.title: Preheat oven and sheet pan
instructions: Place an empty sheet pan in the oven while it preheats (hot pan helps browning).
Optional: line with parchment after removing (be careful—hot pan).

steps.title: Toss vegetables with oil, garlic, and herbs
instructions: In a large bowl, combine vegetables.
Add olive oil, salt, black pepper, thyme, and optional rosemary/red pepper flakes.
Add garlic now for stronger roasted-garlic flavor, OR add it halfway through roasting to reduce scorching risk.
Toss until everything is evenly coated.
checks: Vegetables evenly coated with oil and seasoning
Pieces are cut to similar thickness for even roasting

steps.title: Roast (first stage) and stir
instructions: Carefully remove the hot sheet pan from the oven.
Spread vegetables in a single layer with space between pieces (avoid overcrowding).
Roast 15 minutes, then stir/flip for even browning.

steps.title: Add garlic (if delayed) and finish roasting
instructions: If you held the garlic, sprinkle it over vegetables now and toss quickly on the pan.
Roast 8–12 minutes more, until vegetables are tender with browned edges.
Hard vegetables (carrots) should be fork-tender; broccoli should have crisped tips.

steps.title: Finish with lemon and herbs
instructions: Squeeze fresh lemon juice over the vegetables (start with 1–2 tbsp).
Optional: add a pinch of lemon zest and sprinkle with chopped parsley.

refrigerator.guidance: Cool and store airtight. Best within 2–3 days. Re-crisp in oven/air fryer.

freezer.guidance: Not ideal (texture softens). If freezing, use in soups/stews after thawing.

best: Oven 425°F on a sheet pan 6–10 minutes
Skillet on medium-high with a small oil splash
avoid: Covered microwave reheat (steams and softens browning)

batchNotes: Prep vegetables up to 24 hours ahead and store covered in the fridge (keep broccoli separate if you want peak texture).
Use two pans if doubling to avoid overcrowding.
leftovers: Toss into grain bowls with lemon vinaigrette
Blend into soup base with stock

summary: Sheet-pan Herb & Garlic roasted vegetables with garlic, thyme, olive oil, and a lemon finish—swap-friendly and weeknight reliable.

handling: Use caution with hot sheet pan when removing/adding parchment.
Cool leftovers promptly and refrigerate within 2 hours.

variations.label: All hard vegetables (carrots, potatoes, Brussels sprouts)

notes: Cut into 1-inch chunks and roast 30–40 minutes total, stirring once.
Add garlic at the halfway point for best flavor without scorching.
Roast 15–22 minutes total to avoid over-softening.
Keep pieces larger and avoid overcrowding.
After roasting, sprinkle 1/3 cup grated Parmesan and return to oven 1–2 minutes to melt.
Replace lemon juice with 1–2 tsp balsamic vinegar at finish.
Great with onions, mushrooms, and peppers.
